name: Camp Chaos
white: The lake monster, who is honestly more scared of you.
Bug juice, which is not juice and is possibly bugs.
The counselor's guitar, which knows four chords.
A major capture-the-flag treaty violation.
The raccoon that runs the snack shed now.
The top bunk kid's reign of terror.
Latrine duty.
A care package, minus the counselor tax.
The world's itchiest sleeping bag.
A ghost story that worked way too well.
Tipping the canoe on purpose. Again.
Arts-and-crafts lanyards for the whole family this Christmas.
The camp dance's mandatory six feet of distance.
The kid who writes letters home in secret code.
A tetherball with a personal grudge.
A lake that is 40% frog.
The camp nurse's answer to everything: calamine lotion.
A marshmallow that is entirely on fire.
The talent show's third straight kazoo act.
An archery target that has never been hit, not even once.
The camp director's megaphone at 6 a.m.
Fifty kids singing a song that never ends.
A flashlight that dies at the scariest part.
The buddy system, enforced by whistle.
A friendship bracelet with legally binding powers.
A midnight cabin raid.
Getting a letter at mail call addressed to some other kid.
Wet socks for six straight days.
A trust fall with nobody behind me.
The oldest camper, who has been here since 1998.

black.text: Color War Day Three ended early when the blue team unleashed _.
Campfire legend says the boarded-up cabin on the hill is home to _.
At flag-raising this morning, the camp director gave a very stern speech about _.
Every first-year camper must survive three things: homesickness, the swim test, and _.
Nobody swims in the lake anymore, not since the incident with _.
Next summer's camp brochure promises new bunks, a climbing wall, and absolutely no _.
Lights out means no talking, no flashlights, and definitely no _.
At the end-of-summer awards, "Most Improved" went to _ and "Best Attitude" went to _.
